title: ActiveCampaign vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign and Substack. Learn which email marketing tool suits your business needs, pricing, and features.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs Substack: Detailed Comparison Guide
intro: ActiveCampaign and Substack serve different email marketing purposes, making direct comparison challenging. ActiveCampaign is a comprehensive business automation platform with CRM capabilities, while Substack is a creator-focused newsletter platform with built-in monetization. Your choice depends on whether you need enterprise marketing automation or a simple way to monetize your writing.

comparison_table.tool_b: Free with revenue-share on paid subscriptions
Unlimited free tier with monetization option

comparison_table.tool_a: Advanced automation, CRM, and lead scoring

choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and CRM functionality for your business
You want to implement lead scoring, dynamic content, and sophisticated workflows
You're running an ecommerce store or B2B operation requiring detailed customer tracking
You need SMS capabilities and site tracking integrated with email marketing
choose_b_if: You're a writer, creator, or podcaster wanting to monetize directly from subscribers
You prefer a free platform with no startup costs or credit card required
You want a simple, distraction-free tool for building an audience
You're looking for built-in discovery features and audience recommendations

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they solve different problems. ActiveCampaign excels for businesses needing marketing automation, CRM, and advanced segmentation. Substack is superior for creators seeking simplicity and direct subscriber monetization. Choose based on your primary goal: business growth through automation or content monetization.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper upfront, offering unlimited free use with no subscription required. ActiveCampaign starts at $15/month with no free tier. However, if you monetize on Substack, the platform takes a 10% cut of paid subscriptions, which can become expensive at scale compared to ActiveCampaign's fixed pricing.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber list from Substack to ActiveCampaign, though the process requires manual export and import. ActiveCampaign accepts CSV uploads of your contacts. However, you'll lose Substack's built-in monetization features and discovery mechanisms, so ensure ActiveCampaign's capabilities align with your new strategy.
